data.title: Karolinska Development AB announces a rights issue, not available in certain countries. No offer to acquire securities.
data.description: The following information is restricted from release, publication, or distribution in the United States, Australia, Belarus, Canada, Japan, New Zealand, Russia, Switzerland, Singapore, South Korea, South Africa, or any other jurisdiction where such actions would be unlawful. This press release regarding Karolinska Development AB (publ) should not be considered an offer or invitation to acquire or subscribe for any securities in any jurisdiction. Please see the "Important Information" section for further details.

data.title: CBO predicts Fed rates to drop to 3.4% by 2028 under Trump, with 4.3% Treasury yields and 4.4% unemployment.
data.description: The Federal Reserve is expected to lower short-term rates in 2026, with the key interest rate projected to reach 3.4% by the end of President Trump's term in 2028, as per the Congressional Budget Office (CBO). The CBO report indicates a gradual increase in 10-year Treasury yields, rising from 4.1% in Q4 2025 to 4.3% by Q4 2028. This could lead to higher mortgage borrowing costs in the next two years. The forecast also suggests that the unemployment rate will reach 4.6% in 2026 before declining to 4.4% in 2028 due to Trump's tax and spending policies.

data.description: Kaldalón's Nomination Committee plays an advisory role in the selection of the Company's Board of Directors and nominates candidates for board positions. For more information on the Nomination Committee's role and procedures, please refer to Kaldalón’s website.

data.title: Babylon staking code flaw may slow block production; omitting hash field could crash validators, affecting block creation.
data.description: A recent vulnerability has been uncovered that allows malicious validators to skip the hash field when submitting blocks. This could result in validator crashes and slow down block production. The discovery raises concerns about the security of the system, with potential consequences for block validation and processing efficiency. It is essential for validators to remain vigilant and ensure the inclusion of the hash field in all block submissions to prevent exploitation of this vulnerability. The impact of this issue could be significant, affecting the reliability and performance of the validation process. It is crucial for all validators to be aware of this vulnerability and take necessary precautions to safeguard the integrity of the system.

data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor sees $33 billion revenue in quarter, surpassing forecasts amid AI demand.
data.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSM) exceeded expectations with its financial performance, reporting impressive results for both the month and quarter. During the October–December period, the company generated approximately 1.05 trillion New Taiwanese dollars ($33.1 billion) in revenue, reflecting a notable 20.5% increase from the previous year. This surpassed the analyst consensus of 1.02 trillion New Taiwanese dollars and fell within TSM's guidance range of $32.2 billion to $33.4 billion. The positive performance was attributed to strong demand for artificial intelligence technologies, highlighting significant growth potential in this sector. Executives at Nvidia Corp. also expressed optimism about customer momentum, further supporting TSM's promising outlook.

data.title: South Korea court deems exchange-held Bitcoin seizable under criminal law, aligning with US, EU.
data.description: A recent ruling in South Korea has determined that Bitcoin held in exchange accounts can be seized as evidence under criminal law. This decision brings South Korea in line with enforcement practices seen in the United States and European Union. The ruling highlights the legal implications surrounding cryptocurrency in criminal investigations and signals a growing trend towards regulating digital assets in alignment with traditional financial systems.

data.title: Blockstream Capital Partners buys Numeus Group's division, including Bitcoin trading strategies & 10-person derivatives team.
data.description: A strategic agreement has been reached by Blockstream Capital Partners ("BCP") to acquire a division of Numeus Group's digital asset trading and investment business. The acquisition encompasses select Bitcoin focused trading strategies geared towards yield generation, along with a derivatives trading team of ten members. This team is headed by Chief Investment Officer Deepak Gulati, an expert in volatility and derivatives markets. This acquisition aims to bolster BCP's presence in the digital asset sector, capitalizing on the expertise of the acquired team.
